title: Dipolar interaction-mediated molecular anchoring electrolyte enables wide-temperature sodium-ion batteries with enhanced safety and durability
authors: Yong-Li Heng
Zhen-Yi Gu
Yuan-Zheng Tang
Han-Hao Liu
Xiao-Tong Wang
Jie Wang
Shu-Yu Li
Xing-Long Wu
abstract: Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71861-7 Different challenges in safety, lifespan and temperature adaptability with synergistic interfacial compatibility hinder the development of sodium batteries. Here, this work presents a dipolar interaction-mediated molecular anchoring electrolyte to construct robust interphases for durable wide-temperature (−60 ~ 70 °C) sodium batteries.

title: Suppression of Electroconvection Due to Attractive Forces between Dissolved Polymers and A Metal Electrode
authors: Ankush Mukherjee, Lynden A. Archer and Donald L. Koch
abstract: Electroconvection in rechargeable batteries enhances the growth of non-planar deposits at the electrode surface and this can result in the formation of dendrites during the charging process. Experiments performed by Sharma et al. [Langmuir 2023, 39, 1, 92–100] showed that the addition of low molecular weight polymers to the electrolyte can delay the onset of electroconvection because of the formation of a thin layer of higher polymer concentration adjacent to the electrode. In this study, we develop a model for the formation of such layers due to attractive potential forces such as van der Waa…

title: The Impact of FEC Additive on Deep Eutectic Gel Electrolyte with Self-Healing Property for Solid-State Li Metal Batteries
authors: Qiqi Li, Enfeng Song, Huan Li, Xingjiang Liu and Qiang Xu
abstract: Deep eutectic solvents (DES), as a flame-retardant electrolyte system, have the merit of high ionic conductivity and low preparation cost. However, the poor compatibility of DES-based electrolyte with Li metal anode has seriously restricted its prospects for applications. To solve this problem, a novel DES-based gel electrolyte (DSA) has been prepared by the polymerization of SSH monomer with 4-acryloylmorpholine (ACMO) in the DES solution with/without an additive of fluoroethylene carbonate (FEC) under UV-irradiation, in which SSH monomer is synthesized by the reaction of cystine reagent with…
